executive_summary: A canonical chairman phone-notify capability: one shared helper lib/integrations/todoist/chairman-notify.js exposing notifyChairman({title,description,priority,dueDatetime?,reminder?}) that BOTH Adam and the coordinator invoke uniformly to buzz the chairman's phone for urgent decisions / human action-items. The phone-push is a LAYER on top of the existing coordinator decision-queue / fn_chairman_decide (complement, never replace). The validated push recipe is centralized ONCE: add a Todoist task to the EHG project (6grHWpvVM8QXrj5W) then attach an EXPLICIT v1 reminder via POST https://api.todoist.com/api/v1/sync reminder_add (type:absolute, service:push, due {date: UTC ISO, is_recurring:false}) and VERIFY via a v1 read — because the @doist/todoist-api-typescript SDK (installed, used elsewhere for addTask/getTasks) is BLIND to reminders (a Sync-API-only resource), and dueDatetime / the ! quick-add syntax attach 0 reminders and never push. Follows the existing raw-fetch v1-sync pattern (lib/integrations/post-processor.js item_complete). The capability is encoded canonically: CLAUDE_ADAM.md (regenerated from leo_protocol_sections id=601 adam_role_contract) gains the chairman action-item tracking + Todoist phone-reminder duty; coordinator.md documents when to use it (urgent gate decisions / acceptance deadlines). Safety-first: TODOIST_API_TOKEN read from env, never logged/committed, fail-loud-but-redacted if missing; v1 endpoint ONLY (no deprecated v9); unit tests use a MOCKED fetch (zero real API calls, no chairman spam); the live-buzz recipe is already chairman-validated, so any live check verifies reminder CREATION via v1 read then deletes the test task (no disruptive re-buzz).

functional_requirements.title: Shared notifyChairman helper (task + explicit v1 reminder + verify)
functional_requirements.description: Create lib/integrations/todoist/chairman-notify.js exporting async notifyChairman({title, description, priority=high, dueDatetime?, reminder?}, deps?). Steps: (1) add a Todoist task to the EHG project CHAIRMAN_PROJECT_ID (default 6grHWpvVM8QXrj5W, env-overridable) — via the installed SDK addTask OR a raw item_add; (2) attach an EXPLICIT reminder via raw POST https://api.todoist.com/api/v1/sync, form-encoded commands=[{type:'reminder_add', uuid, args:{type:'absolute', item_id:<taskId>, service:'push', due:{date:<UTC ISO>, is_recurring:false, string:<human>}}}], absolute UTC ISO due (default reminder ~ now+/dueDatetime); (3) VERIFY via a v1 read (sync resource_types:['reminders'] or GET /api/v1/reminders) that the reminder exists for the task; throw fail-loud if sync_status!=ok OR the verify read does not return the reminder. Return {taskId, reminderId, verified:true}. Inject fetch + token via deps for testability (default to global fetch + env). Pure of side effects beyond the two POSTs + read.
acceptance_criteria: notifyChairman adds a task to the EHG project then attaches an explicit v1 reminder_add (type:absolute, service:push, UTC ISO due) and verifies it via a v1 read
throws fail-loud (not silent) if sync_status!=ok or the verify read does not find the reminder
fetch + token are injectable (deps) so unit tests need no real API call

functional_requirements.title: Uniform invocation: npm script + CLI (single entry point)
functional_requirements.description: Add a package.json scripts entry (e.g. "chairman:notify": "node lib/integrations/todoist/chairman-notify.js") so the helper is invokable as a CLI by Adam, the coordinator, and operators uniformly — parsing --title/--description/--priority/--due args and calling notifyChairman. This CLI is also the genuine wire-check entry point for the new lib module (NOT an @wire-check-exempt comment). Both Adam's and the coordinator's runtime callers import notifyChairman directly (no re-implemented reminder logic anywhere).
acceptance_criteria: npm run chairman:notify invokes the helper as a CLI (args -> notifyChairman)
the new lib module is reachable from the package.json-script entry point (wire-check passes without an exemption)
no duplicated v1 reminder_add POST logic exists in any caller (grep-verified) — all route through the helper

functional_requirements.title: Token safety + v1-only + no non-pushing paths
functional_requirements.description: Read the API token from process.env.TODOIST_API_TOKEN; NEVER log or commit it; if missing, fail loud with a redacted message (no token value). Use the v1 endpoint api.todoist.com/api/v1/sync ONLY — no deprecated v9 sync endpoint anywhere. Do NOT rely on dueDatetime or the ! quick-add syntax for the push (both attach 0 reminders); the explicit reminder_add is the only push mechanism. The dueDatetime param, if passed, only sets the reminder's absolute due — it is never used as the sole push trigger.
acceptance_criteria: TODOIST_API_TOKEN read from env, never logged; missing -> fail-loud redacted error
0 references to the v9 sync endpoint; v1 endpoint only (grep-verified)
the push comes from explicit reminder_add (pinned), not dueDatetime/!-syntax

functional_requirements.title: Canonical CLAUDE_ADAM.md duty (leo_protocol_sections + regen)
functional_requirements.description: Edit the leo_protocol_sections row id=601 (section_type='adam_role_contract') to append a clause: Adam tracks chairman HUMAN action-items (.adam-chairman-decisions.json) and routes URGENT chairman decisions via the shared notifyChairman helper (Todoist phone-reminder) in addition to the hourly exec email. Then regenerate via node scripts/generate-claude-md-from-db.js --only CLAUDE_ADAM.md so the generated CLAUDE_ADAM.md reflects it. (DB is the source of truth; CLAUDE_ADAM.md is never hand-edited.)
acceptance_criteria: leo_protocol_sections id=601 content contains the chairman action-item tracking + notifyChairman phone-reminder duty
CLAUDE_ADAM.md regenerated from the DB reflects the new duty (not hand-edited)
the regen command is the canonical generate-claude-md-from-db.js --only CLAUDE_ADAM.md

functional_requirements.title: coordinator.md capability documentation
functional_requirements.description: Add to .claude/commands/coordinator.md (hand-edited slash-command prompt) a documented escalation channel: when a blocking gate decision or an acceptance-sitting deadline is imminent, the coordinator calls notifyChairman() to add a Todoist task + phone reminder — complementing (not replacing) the decision-queue / fn_chairman_decide. State WHEN to use it (urgent only — avoid chairman spam) and HOW (npm run chairman:notify or import the helper).
acceptance_criteria: coordinator.md documents the notifyChairman capability + when to use it (urgent gate decisions / deadlines)
it is framed as a complement to fn_chairman_decide, not a replacement
it cautions against spamming the chairman (urgent only)

functional_requirements.title: Tests (mocked unit + opt-in non-disruptive live verify)
functional_requirements.description: Unit tests with a MOCKED fetch (no real API call, no chairman spam): assert notifyChairman (a) POSTs an item_add/addTask to the EHG project, (b) POSTs reminder_add with type:absolute + service:push + an absolute UTC ISO due, (c) performs the v1 verify read and throws if the reminder is absent, (d) uses the v1 endpoint only (no v9), (e) fails loud + redacted on a missing TODOIST_API_TOKEN, (f) does NOT rely on dueDatetime/!-syntax for the push. PLUS an opt-in live test that self-skips without TODOIST_API_TOKEN and, when run, creates a verified reminder via the helper, confirms it via a v1 read, then DELETES the test task (no disruptive buzz — the live-buzz recipe is pre-validated; this verifies the helper produces a correct verified reminder).
acceptance_criteria: mocked unit tests cover task-add + reminder_add(absolute/push/UTC) + verify-read + v1-only + missing-token + no-dueDatetime-reliance
the live test self-skips without a token and cleans up its test task (no re-buzz)
all tests pass; no real API call in the default unit run

technical_requirements.title: Raw fetch for reminders (SDK is blind)
technical_requirements.description: reminder_add MUST use raw fetch / api.sync() to the v1 sync endpoint (form-encoded commands JSON, Bearer token) — the @doist SDK addTask cannot create reminders. Mirror the existing lib/integrations/post-processor.js v1-sync pattern (uuid + sync_status[uuid]==='ok').

technical_requirements.title: Centralize, do not duplicate
technical_requirements.description: ONE helper owns the task+reminder+verify; Adam and the coordinator import notifyChairman (or call the CLI). No re-implemented reminder_add logic in any caller (the success criterion). post-processor.js (item_complete, a different command) is out of scope for this SD.

technical_requirements.title: Outward-facing safety
technical_requirements.description: Notifies a REAL person (the chairman) on a REAL device. Mocked tests by default; no live buzz in CI/unit; live verify is opt-in + self-cleaning. Token never logged/committed. v1 only.

test_scenarios.expected: task added to the EHG project; reminder_add posted with type:absolute/service:push/UTC-ISO due; v1 verify read confirms the reminder; returns {taskId, reminderId, verified:true}
fail-loud throw (no silent success) — the SDK-blind failure mode is caught
fail-loud, redacted error (no token value, no v9 endpoint)
self-skips without a token; when run, creates a verified reminder then deletes the test task (no disruptive buzz)

acceptance_criteria: Shared notifyChairman helper exists; BOTH Adam and the coordinator invoke it (no duplicated v1-POST logic in either caller)
A reminder created via the helper attaches a verified v1 push reminder (verify-via-v1) — the validated recipe preserved (no disruptive re-buzz needed)
CLAUDE_ADAM.md (generated from leo_protocol_sections id=601) contains the chairman action-item tracking + notifyChairman phone-reminder duty
coordinator.md documents the capability + when to use it (urgent gate decisions), complementing fn_chairman_decide
Helper uses the v1 endpoint only (no v9); token never logged; tests pass with a mocked fetch (no chairman spam); adversarial review clean

risks.risk: Reminder silently fails to attach (SDK blind to v1 reminders) -> chairman never buzzed
risks.mitigation: explicit reminder_add + verify-via-v1 read; fail loud if absent (TS-2)
env-only; never logged; redacted errors; pre-commit secret scan

risks.risk: Spamming / disturbing the chairman during build/test
risks.mitigation: mocked-fetch unit tests; live verify opt-in + deletes its test task; recipe pre-validated

risks.risk: Deprecated v9 endpoint or dueDatetime/!-syntax (0 reminders, no push)
risks.mitigation: v1 reminder_add only; pinned tests assert v1 + explicit reminder

risks.risk: Duplicated reminder logic drifts between Adam + coordinator
risks.mitigation: single shared helper imported by both; grep-verified

system_architecture.summary: One shared lib helper (chairman-notify.js) centralizes the task-add + explicit v1 reminder_add + verify-via-v1 recipe (raw fetch, SDK is blind to reminders), exposed as an npm-script CLI that is the wire-check entry point. The duty is encoded canonically in the DB (leo_protocol_sections id=601 -> CLAUDE_ADAM.md regen) and in coordinator.md. Phone-push layers on top of fn_chairman_decide.

components.name: lib/integrations/todoist/chairman-notify.js
components.change: NEW: notifyChairman (task + v1 reminder_add + verify), injectable fetch/token, CLI main (FR-1/2/3)

components.name: leo_protocol_sections id=601 (adam_role_contract) + CLAUDE_ADAM.md
components.change: EDIT row + regen --only CLAUDE_ADAM.md: chairman action-item tracking + notify duty (FR-4)

components.name: .claude/commands/coordinator.md
components.change: MODIFY: document the notify capability + when to use (FR-5)

components.name: tests/unit/integrations/chairman-notify.test.js (+ opt-in live)
components.change: NEW: mocked-fetch unit pins + self-skipping live verify (FR-6)

implementation_approach.summary: Build the shared helper using the existing raw-fetch v1-sync pattern (post-processor.js) extended to reminder_add (absolute/push/UTC ISO) + verify-via-v1; expose it as an npm-script CLI (wire-check entry point); centralize so both Adam + coordinator import it; encode the duty in the DB (leo_protocol_sections id=601 -> regen CLAUDE_ADAM.md) + coordinator.md; pin with mocked-fetch unit tests + an opt-in self-cleaning live verify; adversarially review the recipe correctness + token safety + the SDK-blind failure mode before merge; never spam the chairman.
steps: chairman-notify.js (task + reminder_add + verify, injectable deps, CLI)
mocked-fetch unit tests + opt-in live verify

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Run the mocked-fetch unit suite for chairman-notify
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: notifyChairman adds a task to the EHG project then posts an explicit v1 reminder_add (type:absolute, service:push, absolute UTC ISO due) and verifies via a v1 read; throws fail-loud if the reminder is absent; uses the v1 endpoint only; fails redacted on a missing token; does not rely on dueDatetime/!-syntax; zero real API calls

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Grep all callers (Adam + coordinator) + the codebase for inline reminder_add / v9 endpoint usage
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: 0 duplicated reminder_add POST blocks (all route through the helper); 0 references to the deprecated v9 sync endpoint

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Confirm CLAUDE_ADAM.md (regenerated from leo_protocol_sections id=601) + coordinator.md document the capability
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: CLAUDE_ADAM.md contains the chairman action-item tracking + notifyChairman Todoist phone-reminder duty; coordinator.md documents when to use it for urgent gate decisions (complement to fn_chairman_decide)

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Run the opt-in live verify (only if TODOIST_API_TOKEN is set)
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: creates a verified v1 reminder via the helper, confirms it via a v1 read, then deletes the test task; self-skips cleanly if no token (no disruptive buzz)
